nodes.label: AI as brainstorming and idea generator
nodes.label_negative: AI as replacement for hand-drawn art
nodes.grounding_span: I'll ask AI tools such as Gemini or ChatGPT to help me brainstorm some ideas

nodes.label: AI as skill gap filler
nodes.label_negative: AI as crutch hindering skill development
nodes.grounding_span: I'm just using AI to fill the skill gaps I still have until I improve my craft enough to confidently fill those gaps on my own

nodes.label: AI as compromise to creative integrity
nodes.label_negative: Unethical AI use (stealing, selling generated art as own)
nodes.grounding_span: I'm not stealing work, I'm not using the images themselves as the assets, and I'm only using the images as inspiration instead of the end product

nodes.label: Hand-drawn artistic authenticity
nodes.grounding_span: I want to make 'real' art, using my own ideas, designs, creativity, my own hand drawing the artwork

nodes.label: AI as rapid asset generator
nodes.grounding_span: I'm worried that the AI-generated assets will oversaturate the market and take exposure away from creators who design things by hand

nodes.label: AI as supplement to creative process
nodes.grounding_span: It would be nice to switch instead to an AI tool to help me organize and name files

nodes.label: Personal creative skill development
nodes.grounding_span: I'm just using AI to fill the skill gaps I still have until I improve my craft enough to confidently fill those gaps on my own

nodes.label: Creative integrity and human artistry
nodes.grounding_span: I want to make 'real' art, using my own ideas, designs, creativity, my own hand drawing the artwork

nodes.label: Market access and fair competition
nodes.grounding_span: I'm worried that the AI-generated assets will oversaturate the market and take exposure away from creators who design things by hand

nodes.label: Productivity and efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: Automation would really speed up the process and allow me to spend more time creating

nodes.label: Guilt about secret AI use
nodes.grounding_span: It felt like cheating at first, to use AI to come up with a style that I don't have the skill to create on my own

nodes.label: Acceptance of AI as ethical learning tool
nodes.grounding_span: When I realized that my use of AI isn't making use of these unethical practices, I felt less guilty

nodes.label: Concern about market oversaturation by AI art
nodes.grounding_span: I'm worried that the AI-generated assets will oversaturate the market and take exposure away from creators who design things by hand

nodes.label: Aspiration to stop using generative AI after skill improvement
nodes.grounding_span: I'd like to use AI to fill my gaps in creative skill until I improve enough to go through the entire design process without help. Then, I'd like to stop using generative AI

nodes.label: Pragmatic acceptance of AI as a tool
nodes.grounding_span: Realistically, I'm worried I'll need to keep using generative AI and even start selling generated content just to keep up in the marketplace so I can make a living

nodes.label: Ethical boundary-setting around AI use
nodes.grounding_span: I'm not stealing work, I'm not using the images themselves as the assets, and I'm only using the images as inspiration instead of the end product

nodes.label: Learning through imitation and modification — traces AI outputs to internalize skills
nodes.grounding_span: I've spent so much time looking at these generated images and tracing the shapes and altering those shapes to be what I envisioned
